content: Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand my role and the current state of the project.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

Iter-066 through iter-068 delivered minimal net closure: BasicOpenCech remained at 15 syntactic sorries, Differentials at 7. 13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16

After iter-068, Phase A's `BasicOpenCech.lean` has **15 syntactic sorries** in `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`; Phase B's `Differentials.lean` has **7 syntactic sorries**; Phase C's `Jacobian.lean` has **5 protected sorries** now in active scope; Phase D's `Genus.lean` is closed; Phase E's `AbelJacobi.lean` has **3 protected sorries** deferred until `Jacobian C` lands.

This is the scheme-language version of `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule`: for `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Globalise via `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafFor` / restrict-to-basis machinery.

The best viable alternative is the **Albanese-functor representability framework** combined with **symmetric-power infrastructure**:
47	   - Define the Albanese functor: for a pointed curve `(C, P)`, the functor sending a pointed abelian variety `(A, a)` over `k` to the set of pointed morphisms `C → A` sending `P` to `a`.
48	   - Show that a representing object for this functor would simultaneously deliver `Jacobian C` (the representing scheme), `ofCurve P` (the universal pointed morphism), `comp_ofCurve` (pointed property), and `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (universal property).

54	## Current Objectives
55	
56	### 1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`** — close one or more sorries inside `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`

79	#### What success looks like
80	
81	**(Best)** One or more sorries fully closed. Syntactic-sorry trajectory `15 → 14` or better. Closing `h_π_split` or L495 would be highest impact.
82	
83	**(Good)** Substantial new scaffolding landed (e.g., `Pi.module` instances drafted with explicit transport; `ExtraDegeneracy` instance constructed for substep (a); short-exact-sequence `ker(π) → K → K₀` packaged as a `ShortComplex`). Trajectory unchanged or +1 from finer decomposition.

89	### 2. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean`** — attack `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`, or `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` substep 2
90	
91	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
92	
93	Iter-065 prover report (**recommended pre-read**): `logs/iter-065/prover-Differentials-result.md` — contains the `moduleKPresheafOfModules` cluster fix details, the `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` decomposition, and iter-066 next-step recommendations.
94	
95	**Action**: Attack one of the three highest-priority closures:
96	
97	1. **`cotangentExactSeqAlpha` (L199)** — the base-change cotangent map `f^* Ω_{Y/S} → Ω_{X/S}`. For a composition `X → Y → S`, on an affine chart with rings `A = O_S(W)`, `A' = O_Y(f(V))`, `B = O_X(V)`, this is `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`: `Ω_{A'/A} ⊗_{A'} B → Ω_{B/A}`. The global construction glues these local maps using `PresheafOfModules.homMk` applied to the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange`.
98	
99	2. **`cotangentExactSeqBeta` (L218)** — the relative-quotient cotangent map `Ω_{X/S} → Ω_{X/Y}`. Locally, this is `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B`: `Ω_{B/A} → Ω_{B/A'}`. The global construction follows from the morphism of `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'` induced by the natural transformation `(f ≫ g)⁻¹ O_S → f⁻¹ O_Y` between the pullback ring presheaves.
100	
101	3. **Substep 2 of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` (L113)** — A scheme-language localisation-compatibility lemma. For an affine open `Spec B ↪ X` with structure ring map `A → B` (induced by the morphism `f : X → S`), and for each `g ∈ B`, the sections of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over the basic open `D(g)` are isomorphic to `Ω_{B[1/g]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/g]`. Mathlib leverage: `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic`), `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (the ring-map version). The sheaf condition on the basis of basic opens then follows by descent. **This is a multi-iteration effort (~200-400 LOC).** Iter-069 may land scaffolding + helper lemmas + draft a localisation-compatibility theorem for one basic open without fully closing the sheaf condition; transient sorries are acceptable.
102	
103	The prover may also opportunistically attack other sorries (`smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, `cotangent_at_section`, `serre_duality_genus`) if the session permits, but **items 1–3 are the priority**.
104	
105	#### Notes for the prover
106	
107	- **File**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` only. Do not modify any other `.lean` file.
108	- **Mathematical content**: in `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
109	- **Mathlib leverage**:
110	  - `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic`) — ring-level base-change cotangent map.
111	  - `KaehlerDifferential.map` — ring-level relative-quotient cotangent map.
112	  - `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.CotangentComplex`) — the ring-level cotangent exact sequence (composition zero, exactness, surjectivity).
113	  - `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` / `isLocalizedModule_map` — localisation compatibility.

134	
135	**The best viable route**: the **Albanese universal property** combined with the **symmetric-power / divisor-class** construction.
136	
137	Mathematically, for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k` with a `k`-rational point `P`, the Jacobian `Jac(C)` is the abelian variety that represents the functor
138	
139	```
140	T ↦ { pointed morphisms C_T → A_T over T | P ↦ 0 }
141	```
142	
143	for variable abelian varieties `A` over `k`. Equivalently, `Jac(C)` is the connected component of the identity of the Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}`, which parametrizes degree-0 line bundles on `C`.
144	
145	Since the Picard-scheme route is blocked, the prover should begin the **Albanese-functor route**:
146	
147	1. **Define the Albanese functor** `AlbaneseFunctor C P : (Over (Spec k))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` that sends a `k`-scheme `T` to the set of pairs `(A, f)` where `A` is an abelian variety over `T` and `f : C_T → A` is a morphism sending the `T`-point induced by `P` to the identity section of `A`. (Or, more simply, define it as the functor of pointed morphisms to a fixed abelian variety and then vary the abelian variety.)
148	
149	   A more tractable first step: define a predicate `IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec k)) (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) (J : Over (Spec k)) [GrpObj J] : Prop` that says `J` satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. This predicate bundles the data of `ofCurve P : C ⟶ J` and the universal property `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`.
150	
151	2. **Show that `IsAlbanese` characterizes `J` uniquely up to isomorphism.** If `IsAlbanese C P J₁` and `IsAlbanese C P J₂`, then there is a unique isomorphism `J₁ ≅ J₂` compatible with the Abel–Jacobi maps. This uses the rigidity theorem (`AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean`, closed iter-002).
152	
153	3. **Attempt to construct `Jacobian C` from `IsAlbanese`.** If the prover can prove `∃ J, IsAlbanese C P J`, then `Jacobian C` can be defined as `Classical.choose` of this existential. The existence proof would go via the symmetric-power route: for `g = genus C`, the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g(C)` (or the `g`-fold product `C^g` modulo the symmetric group action) maps surjectively to `Pic^g(C)` via the Abel–Jacobi map; tensoring with the fixed line bundle `O(gP)^{-1}` identifies `Pic^g(C) ≅ Pic^0(C) = Jac(C)`.
154	
155	   Since Mathlib does not have symmetric powers of schemes or quotients by finite group actions, the prover should **define the necessary infrastructure**:

158	   - The identification `Pic^g ≅ Pic^0` via the fixed point `P`.

16	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes
17	
18	This file constructs the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}`
19	of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S`, together with its universal derivation
20	`d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. It also states the cotangent exact sequence and the
21	characterisation of smoothness in terms of local freeness of `Ω`.
22	
23	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
24	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
25	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 064 — scaffold)
28	
29	All main declarations have `sorry` bodies. Closure trajectory is estimated
30	at ~10 iterations per `STRATEGY.md`.

37	set_option autoImplicit false
38	
39	universe u
40

42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
44	
45	variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}

47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/
48	
49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
63	
64	/-! ### Sheaf condition: decomposition
65	
66	Closing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` is the main outstanding
67	gap of Phase B step 1. The mathematical proof has three layers:
68	
69	1. **`KaehlerDifferential` commutes with localisation.** For a ring map
70	   `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Mathlib's
71	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (and the scheme variant
72	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`) provides this.
73	2. **The presheaf is a sheaf on the basis of affine basic opens.** On a
74	   basic open `D(f)` of `Spec B`, sections are `Ω_{B[1/f]/A}`. Gluing on a
75	   basic-open cover `{D(fᵢ)}` of `Spec B` follows from the localisation
76	   compatibility of step 1, applied to each `fᵢ`.
77	3. **Globalisation to all opens.** A presheaf on a scheme is a sheaf iff
78	   it is a sheaf on the basis of affine opens (more precisely, on the
79	   basic opens of every affine open). This is the standard scheme
80	   sheaf-of-modules construction.
81	
82	The substep theorems below are the natural decomposition. Each remains
83	`sorry` pending the full proof; the structure mirrors the blueprint at
84	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`. -/
85	
86	/-- Substep 1 (localisation compatibility): For an affine open `V` of `X`
87	mapped to an affine open `U` of `S`, the sections of
88	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` over `V` are isomorphic (as additive groups)
89	to the Kähler differential module `KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app (op V))`. -/
90	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
91	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
92	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
93	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
94	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
95	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
96	  rfl
97	
98	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials is a **sheaf** in the Zariski
99	topology: Kähler differentials commute with localisation, which gives the
100	gluing axiom on affine opens.
101	
102	**Status (iter-065):** Sorry. Proof requires combining:
103	- Substep 1 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler`, definitional)
104	- Substep 2: sheaf condition on the basis of basic opens, derived from
105	  `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule`
106	- Substep 3: globalisation from a basis to all opens (Mathlib has
107	  `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and `isSheafFor` lemmas
108	  for restricting to a basis)
109	
110	The route via `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` is not viable since that
111	requires the result we are trying to prove (the presheaf must already be
112	a sheaf to be packaged as a `SheafOfModules`). -/
113	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
114	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
115	      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by
116	  -- Strategy: reduce to the underlying presheaf of types via
117	  -- `isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp`, then verify the sheaf condition on
118	  -- affine opens using `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`.
119	  -- The proof requires substantial development of a
120	  -- "sheaf condition restricted to affine basis" lemma + the localisation
121	  -- compatibility for Kähler differentials in the scheme-language.
122	  sorry
123	
124	/-- The sheaf of relative differentials `Ω_{X/S}`.
125	
126	Packages the presheaf `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` with the sheaf axiom
127	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` into the standard `X.Modules` shape. -/
128	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
129	  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩
130	
131	/-! ## Universal derivation -/
132	
133	/-- The universal derivation `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`.
134	
135	On each affine open `V = Spec B` over `Spec A`, this is the Mathlib universal
136	derivation `d_{B/A} : B → Ω_{B/A}`. It is an `A`-linear sheaf map satisfying
137	the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a d(b) + b d(a)`. -/
138	noncomputable def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
139	    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
140	      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
141	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
142	    f.c
143	  let d' := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ'
144	  have h_eq : X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := rfl
145	  refine {
146	    app := fun U ↦ AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (d'.d (X := U)),
147	    naturality := fun U V g ↦ ?naturality
148	  }
149	  case naturality =>
150	    ext x
151	    simp only [sheafCompose_obj_obj, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj,
152	      CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj, Functor.comp_map, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
153	    suffices d'.d ((ConcreteCategory.hom (X.presheaf.map g)) x) =
154	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).map g)) (d'.d x) by
155	      simpa using this
156	    exact d'.d_map g x

158	/-! ## Cotangent exact sequence
159	
160	The cotangent exact sequence
161	```
162	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0
163	```
164	for a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S` is decomposed into five named sub-claims:
165	
166	1. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` — the base-change cotangent map
167	   `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`. Locally, this is
168	   `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`, where `A → A' → B` is the
169	   tower of structure ring maps on an affine chart.
170	2. `cotangentExactSeqBeta` — the relative-quotient cotangent map
171	   `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`. Locally, this is the surjection
172	   `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` induced by the inclusion `A → A'`

279	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/

281	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
282	    Smooth f ↔
283	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧

293	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
294	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
295	`k`-vector space. -/

297	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
298	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
299	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :

301	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
302	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)

306	/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/
307	
308	/-- The `O_C(U)`-module structure on `M(U)` viewed as a `k`-module via
309	restriction of scalars along the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. -/
310	noncomputable abbrev moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj
311	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
312	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
313	    (M : C.left.Modules)
314	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{u} k :=
315	  (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
316	
317	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
318	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
319	restriction of scalars. -/
320	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
321	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
322	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
323	    (M : C.left.Modules)
324	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
325	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
326	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
327	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
328	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
329	  -- naturality.
330	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
331	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
332	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
333	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
334	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
335	  -- Now goal:
336	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
337	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
338	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
339	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
340	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
341	    ext x
342	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
343	  rw [hpres]
344	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
345	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)
346	  -- hnat has smul on the *target* of (M.val.map f) at index (kU r).
347	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
348	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).
349	  -- By algebraMap_naturality, (R.map f).hom (kU r) = (kV r), which is what we need.
350	  have halg : (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
351	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
352	      (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r := by
353	    rw [← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection, ← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
354	    exact toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r
355	  -- Convert hnat's target smul to the desired form via halg
356	  have htarget : ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars
357	        (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))).obj (M.val.obj V)).smul
358	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
359	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := by
360	    change (M.val.obj V).smul ((RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
361	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)) =
362	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r)
363	    rw [halg]
364	  rw [htarget] at hnat
365	  exact hnat
366	
367	/-- The restriction map for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`, extracted as a separate
368	definition so the elaborator does not have to inline the smul-naturality proof
369	inside the main `Functor` definition. -/
370	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
371	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
372	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
373	    (M : C.left.Modules)
374	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
375	    moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U ⟶ moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V :=
376	  ModuleCat.homMk (M.val.presheaf.map f) (moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat C M f)
377	
378	@[simp] lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂
379	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
380	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
381	    (M : C.left.Modules)
382	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
383	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map
384	        (moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f) = M.val.presheaf.map f := by
385	  unfold moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
386	  exact ModuleCat.forget₂_map_homMk _ _
387	
388	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
389	scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`.
390	
391	On each open `U`, the sections are the `O_C(U)`-module `M(U)` viewed as a
392	`k`-module via the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. The restriction maps are `k`-linear
393	by the naturality of this algebra map. -/
394	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
395	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
396	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
397	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
398	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
399	  obj U := moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U
400	  map f := moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f
401	  map_id U := by
402	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
403	    rw [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
404	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_id U
405	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
406	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
407	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
408	    simp only [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂]
409	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp f g
410	
411	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
412	its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
413	`O_C`-module. -/
414	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
415	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
416	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
417	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
418	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
419	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
420	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
421	  convert M.isSheaf using 1
422	
423	/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
424	scalars along the structure morphism. -/
425	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
426	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
427	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
428	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
429	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
430	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
431	
432	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
433	
434	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
435	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
436	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
437	
438	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
439	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
440	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
441	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
442	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
443	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
444	      Module.rank k
445	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
446	  sorry
447	
448	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

1	\chapter{The sheaf of relative differentials}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the sheaf of relative K\"ahler differentials
5	$\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $X \to S$, starting from the
6	ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already
7	available in Mathlib. The output is a quasi-coherent sheaf of
8	$\struct{X}$-modules characterised by its restriction to affine opens:
9	for any affine open $V = \Spec B \subseteq X$ lying over an affine open
10	$U = \Spec A \subseteq S$, the sections satisfy
11	\[
12	  \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{B/A}.
13	\]
14	
15	The downstream role of this chapter is twofold. First, the smoothness
16	hypothesis on the curve $C \to \Spec k$ that appears in the Jacobian
17	definition and the Abel--Jacobi map amounts to the condition that
18	$\Omega_{C/k}$ is locally free of rank one; this characterisation is
19	what unlocks the dimension computation
20	$\genus(C) = \dim_k H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C})$.
21	Second, the cotangent space at the identity section of $\Jac(C)$ is
22	$\Omega_{\Jac(C)/k}$ pulled back along the unit $e \colon \Spec k \to
23	\Jac(C)$; the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ that appears in the
24	smoothness statement is read off from this tangent space.
25	
26	\section{The relative cotangent sheaf}
27	
28	\begin{definition}\leanok
29	[Relative cotangent presheaf of an algebra]
30	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
31	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
32	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. For each pair of
33	  affine opens $U \subseteq S$, $V \subseteq X$ with $f(V) \subseteq U$,
34	  write $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$, and use the
35	  $f$-induced ring map $A \to B$. The K\"ahler differential module
36	  $\Omega_{B/A}$ is then a $B$-module, hence a section over $V$ of a
37	  presheaf on $X$. The collection of these modules, together with the
38	  natural restriction maps along inclusions of affine opens, defines a
39	  presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$.
40	\end{definition}
41	
42	\begin{definition}\leanok
43	[Relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$]
44	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
45	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentials}
46	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
47	  The presheaf above is in fact a sheaf in the Zariski topology on
48	  $X$: the K\"ahler differential module commutes with localisation,
49	  which gives the gluing axiom on affine opens, and the descent to
50	  non-affine opens is standard. The resulting sheaf of
51	  $\struct{X}$-modules is the \emph{sheaf of relative differentials}
52	  $\Omega_{X/S}$. It is quasi-coherent.
53	\end{definition}
54	
55	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
56	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
57	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module
58	  $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d}
59	  \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on
60	  affine opens is, by definition, this Mathlib module. The
61	  derivative-of-localisation isomorphism $\Omega_{B[1/f]/A} \cong
62	  \Omega_{B/A} \otimes_B B[1/f]$ is precisely what makes the
63	  affine-restriction presheaf into a sheaf.
64	\end{remark}
65	
66	\section{Universal property and the cotangent exact sequence}
67	
68	\begin{definition}\leanok
69	[Universal derivation]
70	  \label{def:universal_derivation}
71	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.universalDerivation}
72	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
73	  The morphism of sheaves of abelian groups
74	  $\mathrm{d} \colon \struct{X} \to \Omega_{X/S}$, defined on each
75	  affine open $V = \Spec B$ over $\Spec A$ by the Mathlib universal
76	  derivation $\mathrm{d}_{B/A} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$, is the
77	  \emph{universal derivation}. It is an $A$-linear sheaf map satisfying
78	  the Leibniz rule $\mathrm{d}(ab) = a \mathrm{d}(b) + b \mathrm{d}(a)$.
79	\end{definition}
80	
81	% NOTE (iter-065 review): the Lean statement contains a `(by sorry)` inside
82	% the `CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β (by sorry)` argument; the existential
83	% bundle therefore does not yet assert $\alpha \circ \beta = 0$ mathematically
84	% until the nested sorry is closed (or the statement is re-shaped to expose
85	% the composition-zero proof obligation as a separate slot).
86	\begin{theorem}\leanok
87	[Cotangent exact sequence for a composition]
88	  \label{thm:cotangent_exact_sequence}
89	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_exact_sequence}
90	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf, def:universal_derivation}
91	  For schemes $X \xrightarrow{f} Y \xrightarrow{g} S$, there is an
92	  exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
93	  \[
94	    f^*\Omega_{Y/S} \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\longrightarrow\;
95	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0,
96	  \]
97	  built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
98	  sequence and glued via the compatibility above.
99	\end{theorem}
100	
101	\section{Smoothness and local freeness of $\Omega$}
102	
103	\begin{theorem}\leanok
104	[Smoothness in terms of $\Omega$]
105	  \label{thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
106	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
107	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
108	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a finite-presentation morphism of schemes.
109	  Then $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ if and only if
110	  $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $n$ as a sheaf of
111	  $\struct{X}$-modules. The forward direction is the Jacobian
112	  criterion; the converse follows from the cotangent exact sequence
113	  and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point.
114	\end{theorem}
115	
116	\begin{corollary}\leanok
117	[Cotangent space at a section]
118	  \label{cor:cotangent_at_section}
119	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_at_section}
120	  \uses{thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
121	  If $f \colon X \to S$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and
122	  $s \colon S \to X$ is a section of $f$, then the cotangent space
123	  $s^*\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$.
124	  In particular, if $S = \Spec k$ is a field point and $X$ is smooth of
125	  relative dimension $n$, the cotangent space at the corresponding
126	  $k$-point is an $n$-dimensional $k$-vector space.
127	\end{corollary}
128	
129	\section{Curves and the canonical sheaf}
130	
131	The case of a smooth proper curve $C \to \Spec k$ is the special case
132	$n = 1$: the relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{C/k}$ is a line bundle
133	on $C$, classically denoted $\omega_C$ and called the \emph{canonical
134	sheaf}.
135	
136	\begin{theorem}\leanok
137	[Serre-duality genus equality]
138	  \label{thm:serre_duality_genus}
139	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.serre_duality_genus}
140	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_sheaf}
141	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a
142	  field $k$. Then
143	  \[
144	    \dim_k H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C}).
145	  \]
146	  Both sides equal the genus $\genus(C)$. The equality is the dimension
147	  one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical sheaf; combined
148	  with the definition $\genus(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \struct{C})$, this
149	  provides the alternative differential-theoretic characterisation used
150	  when computing the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ at the identity.
151	\end{theorem}

16	Given a colimit cocone `cR` for a presheaf of rings `R` on a cofiltered category `C`,
17	`M` a presheaf of modules over `R`, and a colimit cocone `cM` for the underlying
18	functor `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` of `M`, we define a structure of module over `cR.pt`
19	on a type-synonym `PresheafOfModules.ModuleColimit` for `cM.pt`. This extends to
20	a functor `PresheafOfModules.colimitFunctor : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat cR.pt`.

264	@[simp]
265	lemma homEquiv_app_apply {N : ModuleCat.{w} cR.pt}
266	    (α : ModuleCat.of cR.pt (ModuleColimit hcR hcM) ⟶ N) {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x : M.obj X) :
267	    dsimp% (homEquiv hcR hcM α).app X x = α (cM.ι.app X x) :=
268	  rfl
269	
270	lemma homEquiv_naturality_right {N N' : ModuleCat.{w} cR.pt}
271	    (φ : ModuleCat.of cR.pt (ModuleColimit hcR hcM) ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ N') :
272	    homEquiv hcR hcM (φ ≫ g) = homEquiv hcR hcM φ ≫ (constFunctor cR).map g := rfl
273	
274	@[simp]
275	lemma homEquiv_symm_apply {N : ModuleCat.{w} cR.pt} (β : M ⟶ (constFunctor cR).obj N)
276	    {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x : M.obj X) :
277	    dsimp% (homEquiv hcR hcM).symm β (cM.ι.app X x) = β.app X x := by
278	  exact homEquiv'_symm_apply ..
279	
280	section
281	
282	variable {M' : PresheafOfModules.{w} R} {cM' : Cocone M'.presheaf}
283	  (hcM' : IsColimit cM')
284	
285	/-- The linear map between the colimit modules induced by a morphism of modules. -/
286	noncomputable def map (f : M ⟶ M') :
287	    ModuleColimit hcR hcM →ₗ[cR.pt] ModuleColimit hcR hcM' where
288	  toFun := hcM.desc ((Cocone.precompose ((toPresheaf _).map f)).obj cM')
289	  map_add' _ _ := map_add _ _ _
290	  map_smul' r m := by
291	    obtain ⟨U, r, m, rfl, rfl⟩ := ModuleColimit.jointly_surjective₂ r m
292	    let c := (Cocone.precompose ((toPresheaf _).map f)).obj cM'
293	    have h₁ := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (hcM.fac c U) (r • m)
294	    have h₂ := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (hcM.fac c U) m
295	    dsimp [c] at h₁ h₂ ⊢
296	    rw [ModuleColimit.smul_eq]
297	    erw [h₁, h₂, ModuleColimit.smul_eq, ← (f.app U).hom.map_smul]
298	    rfl
299	
300	@[simp]
301	lemma map_apply (f : M ⟶ M') {U : Cᵒᵖ} (m : M.obj U) :
302	    dsimp% map hcR hcM hcM' f (ιM m) = ιM (f.app _ m) :=
303	  ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (hcM.fac ((Cocone.precompose ((toPresheaf _).map f)).obj cM') U) m
304	
305	@[simp]
306	lemma map_id : map hcR hcM hcM (𝟙 M) = .id := by
307	  ext m
308	  obtain ⟨U, m, rfl⟩ := ιM_jointly_surjective m
309	  simp
310	
311	lemma comp_map
312	    (f : M ⟶ M')
313	    {M'' : PresheafOfModules.{w} R} {cM'' : Cocone M''.presheaf}
314	    (hcM'' : IsColimit cM'') (g : M' ⟶ M'') :
315	    (map hcR hcM' hcM'' g).comp (map hcR hcM hcM' f) = map hcR hcM hcM'' (f ≫ g) := by
316	  ext m
317	  obtain ⟨U, m, rfl⟩ := ιM_jointly_surjective m
318	  simp
319	
320	end
321	
322	lemma homEquiv_naturality_left {M' : PresheafOfModules.{w} R} {cM' : Cocone M'.presheaf}
323	    (hcM' : IsColimit cM') {N : ModuleCat.{w} cR.pt}
324	    (φ' : ModuleCat.of cR.pt (ModuleColimit hcR hcM') ⟶ N)
325	    (f : M ⟶ M') :
326	    homEquiv hcR hcM (ModuleCat.ofHom (map hcR hcM hcM' f) ≫ φ') =
327	      f ≫ homEquiv hcR hcM' φ' := by
328	  ext U m
329	  simp only [homEquiv_app_apply, ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_comp,
330	    Function.comp_apply, comp_app]
331	  apply congr_arg
332	  exact map_apply hcR hcM hcM' f m
333	
334	lemma homEquiv_naturality_left_symm {M' : PresheafOfModules.{w} R} {cM' : Cocone M'.presheaf}
335	    (hcM' : IsColimit cM') {N : ModuleCat.{w} cR.pt}
336	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : M' ⟶ (constFunctor cR).obj N) :
337	    (homEquiv hcR hcM).symm (f ≫ g) =
338	      ModuleCat.ofHom (map hcR hcM hcM' f) ≫ (homEquiv hcR hcM').symm g :=
339	  (homEquiv hcR hcM).injective (by
340	    obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := (homEquiv hcR hcM').surjective g
341	    simp [homEquiv_naturality_left])
342	
343	end ModuleColimit
344	
345	end
346	
347	/-- The colimit module functor from the category of presheaves of modules
348	over a presheaf of rings `R` on a cofiltered category to the category
349	of modules over a colimit of `R`. -/
350	noncomputable def colimitFunctor : PresheafOfModules.{w} R ⥤ ModuleCat.{w} cR.pt where
351	  obj M := ModuleCat.of _ (ModuleColimit hcR (colimit.isColimit M.presheaf))
352	  map f := ModuleCat.ofHom (ModuleColimit.map _ _ _ f)
353	  map_comp f g := by ext : 1; exact (ModuleColimit.comp_map ..).symm
354	
355	/-- Given a presheaf of rings `R` on a cofiltered category, this is the
356	adjunction between `colimitFunctor : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat cR.pt`
357	and the constant functor. -/
358	noncomputable def colimitAdjunction :
359	    colimitFunctor.{w} hcR ⊣ constFunctor.{w} cR :=
360	  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv
361	    { homEquiv _ _ := (ModuleColimit.homEquiv _ _).toEquiv
362	      homEquiv_naturality_left_symm _ _ := ModuleColimit.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm _ _ _ _ _
363	      homEquiv_naturality_right _ _ := ModuleColimit.homEquiv_naturality_right _ _ _ _ }
364	
365	lemma colimitAdjunction_homEquiv
366	    (F : PresheafOfModules R) (G : ModuleCat cR.pt) :
367	    dsimp% (colimitAdjunction.{w} hcR).homEquiv F G =
368	      (ModuleColimit.homEquiv hcR
369	        (colimit.isColimit F.presheaf)).toEquiv := by
370	  simp [colimitAdjunction]
371	
372	open ModuleColimit in
373	lemma colimitAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_apply
374	    {F : PresheafOfModules R} {G : ModuleCat cR.pt}
375	    (β : F ⟶ (constFunctor cR).obj G) {X : Cᵒᵖ} (m : F.obj X) :
376	    ((colimitAdjunction.{w} hcR).homEquiv F G).symm β
377	      (ModuleColimit.ιM (hcR := hcR) (hcM := colimit.isColimit F.presheaf) m) =
378	        β.app X m := by
379	  rw [colimitAdjunction_homEquiv]
380	  apply homEquiv_symm_apply
381	
382	end PresheafOfModules

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# The presheaf of differentials of a presheaf of modules
13	
14	In this file, we define the type `M.Derivation φ` of derivations
15	with values in a presheaf of `R`-modules `M` relative to
16	a morphism of `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` of presheaves of commutative rings
17	over categories `C` and `D` that are related by a functor `F : C ⥤ D`.
18	We formalize the notion of universal derivation.
19	
20	Geometrically, if `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes (or more generally
21	a morphism of commutative ringed spaces), we would like to apply
22	these definitions in the case where `F` is the pullback functor from
23	open subsets of `S` to open subsets of `X` and `φ` is the
24	morphism $O_S ⟶ f_* O_X$.
25	
26	In order to prove that there exists a universal derivation, the target
27	of which shall be called the presheaf of relative differentials of `φ`,
28	we first study the case where `F` is the identity functor.
29	In this case where we have a morphism of presheaves of commutative
30	rings `φ' : S' ⟶ R`, we construct a derivation
31	`DifferentialsConstruction.derivation'` which is universal.
32	Then, the general case (TODO) shall be obtained by observing that
33	derivations for `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` identify to derivations
34	for `S' ⟶ R` where `S'` is the pullback by `F` of the presheaf of
35	commutative rings `S` (the data is the same: it suffices
36	to show that the two vanishing conditions `d_app` are equivalent).
37	
38	-/
39	
40	@[expose] public section
41	
42	universe v u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
43	
44	open CategoryTheory
45	
46	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
47	
48	namespace PresheafOfModules
49	
50	variable {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {F : C ⥤ D} {S' R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
51	  (M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
52	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) (φ' : S' ⟶ R)
53	
54	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`,
55	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
56	@[ext]
57	structure Derivation where
58	  /-- the underlying additive map `R.obj X →+ M.obj X` of a derivation -/
59	  d {X : Dᵒᵖ} : R.obj X →+ M.obj X
60	  d_mul {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a b : R.obj X) : d (a * b) = a • d b + b • d a := by cat_disch
61	  d_map {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
62	    d (R.map f x) = M.map f (d x) := by cat_disch
63	  d_app {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj X) : d (φ.app X a) = 0 := by cat_disch
64	
65	namespace Derivation
66	
67	-- Note: `d_app` cannot be a simp lemma because `dsimp` would
68	-- simplify the composition of functors `R ⋙ forget₂ _ _`
69	attribute [simp] d_mul d_map
70	
71	variable {M N φ}
72	
73	lemma congr_d {d d' : M.Derivation φ} (h : d = d') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
74	    d.d b = d'.d b := by rw [h]
75	
76	variable (d : M.Derivation φ)
77	
78	@[simp] lemma d_one (X : Dᵒᵖ) : d.d (X := X) 1 = 0 := by
79	  simpa using d.d_mul (X := X) 1 1
80	
81	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
82	/-- The postcomposition of a derivation by a morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
83	@[simps! d_apply]
84	def postcomp (f : M ⟶ N) : N.Derivation φ where
85	  d := (f.app _).hom.toAddMonoidHom.comp d.d
86	  d_map {X Y} g x := by simpa using naturality_apply f g (d.d x)
87	  d_app {X} a := by
88	    dsimp
89	    erw [d_app]
90	    rw [map_zero]
91	
92	/-- The universal property that a derivation `d : M.Derivation φ` must
93	satisfy so that the presheaf of modules `M` can be considered as the presheaf of
94	(relative) differentials of a presheaf of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
95	structure Universal where
96	  /-- An absolute derivation of `M'` descends as a morphism `M ⟶ M'`. -/
97	  desc {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
98	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : M ⟶ M'
99	  fac {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
100	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : d.postcomp (desc d') = d' := by cat_disch
101	  postcomp_injective {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
102	    (φ φ' : M ⟶ M') (h : d.postcomp φ = d.postcomp φ') : φ = φ' := by cat_disch
103	
104	attribute [simp] Universal.fac
105	
106	instance : Subsingleton d.Universal where
107	  allEq h₁ h₂ := by
108	    suffices ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
109	      (d' : M'.Derivation φ), h₁.desc d' = h₂.desc d' by
110	        cases h₁
111	        cases h₂
112	        simp only [Universal.mk.injEq]
113	        ext : 2
114	        apply this
115	    intro M' d'
116	    apply h₁.postcomp_injective
117	    simp
118	
119	end Derivation
120	
121	/-- The property that there exists a universal derivation for
122	a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
123	class HasDifferentials : Prop where
124	  exists_universal_derivation : ∃ (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
125	      (d : M.Derivation φ), Nonempty d.Universal
126	
127	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ R`,
128	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
129	abbrev Derivation' : Type _ := M.Derivation (F := 𝟭 D) φ'
130	
131	namespace Derivation'
132	
133	variable {M φ'}
134	
135	@[simp]
136	lemma d_app (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a : S'.obj X) :
137	    d.d (φ'.app X a) = 0 :=
138	  Derivation.d_app d _
139	
140	/-- The derivation relative to the morphism of commutative rings `φ'.app X` induced by
141	a derivation relative to a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings. -/
142	noncomputable def app (d : M.Derivation' φ') (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X) :=
143	  ModuleCat.Derivation.mk (fun b ↦ d.d b)
144	
145	@[simp]
146	lemma app_apply (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
147	    (d.app X).d b = d.d b := rfl
148	
149	section
150	
151	variable (d : ∀ (X : Dᵒᵖ), (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X))
152	
153	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
154	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ'`, this is the
155	in derivation `M.Derivation' φ'` that is given by a compatible family of derivations
156	with values in the modules `M.obj X` for all `X`. -/
157	def mk (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
158	    (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x)) : M.Derivation' φ' where
159	  d {X} := AddMonoidHom.mk' (d X).d (by simp)
160	
161	variable (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
162	      (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x))
163	
164	@[simp]
165	lemma mk_app (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (mk d d_map).app X = d X := rfl
166	
167	/-- Constructor for `Derivation.Universal` in the case `F` is the identity functor. -/
168	def Universal.mk {d : M.Derivation' φ'}
169	    (desc : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
170	      (_ : M'.Derivation' φ'), M ⟶ M')
171	    (fac : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
172	      (d' : M'.Derivation' φ'), d.postcomp (desc d') = d')
173	    (postcomp_injective : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
174	      (α β : M ⟶ M'), d.postcomp α = d.postcomp β → α = β) : d.Universal where
175	  desc := desc
176	  fac := fac
177	  postcomp_injective := postcomp_injective
178	
179	end
180	
181	end Derivation'
182	
183	namespace DifferentialsConstruction
184	
185	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
186	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials of a morphism of presheaves of
187	commutative rings. -/
188	@[simps -isSimp]
189	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials' :
190	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
191	  obj X := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X)
192	  -- Have to hint `g' := R.map f` below, or it gets unfolded weirdly.
193	  map f := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map (g' := R.map f) (φ'.naturality f)
194	  -- Without `dsimp`, `ext` doesn't pick up the right lemmas.
195	  map_id _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
196	  map_comp _ _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
197	
198	attribute [simp] relativeDifferentials'_obj
199	
200	@[simp]
201	lemma relativeDifferentials'_map_d {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
202	    DFunLike.coe (α := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X))
203	      (β := fun _ ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app Y))
204	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((relativeDifferentials' φ').map f))
205	        (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d x) =
206	        CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d (R.map f x) :=
207	  CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map_d (φ'.naturality f) _
208	
209	/-- The universal derivation. -/
210	noncomputable def derivation' : (relativeDifferentials' φ').Derivation' φ' :=
211	  Derivation'.mk (fun X ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D (φ'.app X))
212	    (fun _ _ f x ↦ (relativeDifferentials'_map_d φ' f x).symm)
213	
214	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
215	/-- The derivation `Derivation' φ'` is universal. -/
216	noncomputable def isUniversal' : (derivation' φ').Universal :=
217	  Derivation'.Universal.mk
218	    (fun {M'} d' ↦
219	      { app := fun X ↦ (d'.app X).desc
220	        naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext (fun b ↦ by
221	          dsimp
222	          rw [ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d, Derivation'.app_apply]
223	          erw [relativeDifferentials'_map_d φ' f]
224	          simp) })
225	    (fun {M'} d' ↦ by
226	      ext X b
227	      apply ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d)
228	    (fun {M} α β h ↦ by
229	      ext1 X
230	      exact CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext (Derivation.congr_d h))
231	
232	instance : HasDifferentials (F := 𝟭 D) φ' := ⟨_, _, ⟨isUniversal' φ'⟩⟩
233	
234	end DifferentialsConstruction
235	
236	end PresheafOfModules

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Generator
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.PartialAdjoint
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.CompositionIso
12	
13	/-!
14	# Pullback of presheaves of modules
15	
16	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a functor, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` be presheaves
17	of rings, and `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` be a morphism of presheaves of rings,
18	we introduce the pullback functor `pullback : PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R`
19	as the left adjoint of `pushforward : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S`.
20	The existence of this left adjoint functor is obtained under suitable universe assumptions.
21	
22	From the compatibility of `pushforward` with respect to composition, we deduce
23	similar pseudofunctor-like properties of the `pullback` functors.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite Functor
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	section
36	
37	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
38	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
39	  [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
40	
41	/-- The pullback functor `PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` induced by
42	a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, defined as the left adjoint
43	functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
44	noncomputable def pullback : PresheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R :=
45	  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint
46	
47	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the adjunction
48	between associated pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
49	of presheaves of modules. -/
50	noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
51	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)
52	
53	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the property
54	that the (partial) left adjoint functor of `pushforward φ` is defined
55	on a certain object `M : PresheafOfModules S`. -/
56	abbrev pullbackObjIsDefined : ObjectProperty (PresheafOfModules.{v} S) :=
57	  (pushforward φ).leftAdjointObjIsDefined
58	
59	end
60	
61	section
62	
63	variable {C D : Type u} [SmallCategory C] [SmallCategory D]
64	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
65	
66	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, where `F : C ⥤ D`,
67	`S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `X : C`, the (partial) left adjoint
68	functor of `pushforward φ` is defined on the object `(free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)`:
69	this object shall be mapped to `(free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))`. -/
70	noncomputable def pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy (X : C) :
71	    (pushforward φ ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)))).CorepresentableBy
72	      ((free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))) where
73	  homEquiv {M} := freeYonedaEquiv.trans
74	    (freeYonedaEquiv (M := (pushforward φ).obj M)).symm
75	  homEquiv_comp {M N} g f := freeYonedaEquiv.injective (by
76	    dsimp
77	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
78	    conv_rhs => erw [freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
79	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
80	    rfl)
81	
82	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda (X : C) :
83	    pullbackObjIsDefined φ ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)) :=
84	  (pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy φ X).isCorepresentable
85	
86	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top :
87	    pullbackObjIsDefined.{u} φ = ⊤ := by
88	  ext M
89	  simp only [Pi.top_apply, Prop.top_eq_true, iff_true]
90	  apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_of_isColimit
91	    M.isColimitFreeYonedaCoproductsCokernelCofork
92	  rintro (_ | _)
93	  all_goals
94	    apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_colimit _
95	      (fun _ ↦ pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda _ _)
96	
97	instance : (pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
98	  isRightAdjoint_of_leftAdjointObjIsDefined_eq_top
99	    (pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top φ)
100	
101	end
102	
103	section
104	
105	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
106	  {E : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} E] {E' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} E']
107	
108	variable {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
109	  {G : D ⥤ E} {T : Eᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ T)
110	
111	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
112	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId.{v} S).symm
113	
114	variable (S) in
115	/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
116	category of presheaves of modules. -/
117	noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
118	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)
119	
120	variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
121	
122	section
123	
124	variable [(pushforward.{v} ψ).IsRightAdjoint]
125	
126	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ)).IsRightAdjoint :=
127	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardComp.{v} φ ψ)
128	
129	/-- The composition of two pullback functors on presheaves of modules identifies
130	to the pullback for the composition. -/
131	noncomputable def pullbackComp :
132	    pullback.{v} φ ⋙ pullback.{v} ψ ≅
133	      pullback.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) :=
134	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
135	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} φ) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} ψ)
136	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ))
137	    (pushforwardComp φ ψ)
138	
139	variable {T' : E'ᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G' : E ⥤ E'} (ψ' : T ⟶ G'.op ⋙ T')
140	  [(pushforward.{v} ψ').IsRightAdjoint]
141	
142	lemma pullback_assoc :
143	    isoWhiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp.{v} ψ ψ') ≪≫
144	      pullbackComp.{v} (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫ whiskerLeft G.op ψ') =
145	    (associator _ _ _).symm ≪≫ isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp.{v} φ ψ) _ ≪≫
146	        pullbackComp.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) ψ' :=
147	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_assoc _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (pushforward_assoc φ ψ ψ')
148	
149	end
150	
151	lemma pullback_id_comp :
152	    pullbackComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
153	      isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId S) (pullback φ) ≪≫ Functor.leftUnitor _ :=
154	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_id_comp _ _ _ _ (pushforward_comp_id φ)
155	
156	lemma pullback_comp_id :
157	    pullbackComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
158	      isoWhiskerLeft _ (pullbackId R) ≪≫ Functor.rightUnitor _ :=
159	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_comp_id _ _ _ _ (pushforward_id_comp φ)
160	
161	end
162	
163	end PresheafOfModules

9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Colimits
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous
11	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
12	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
13	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Bicategory.Adjunction.Adj

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe t u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
32	
33	variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	variable (X) in
36	/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
37	def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf
38	
39	namespace Modules
40	
41	/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))
47	
48	noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
52	
53	section Functor
54	
55	variable (X) in
56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
78	  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
79	
80	end Functor
81	
82	variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}
83	
84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule
93	
94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl
119	
120	@[ext]
121	lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)
125	
126	lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M
127	
128	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
129	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
133	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]
136	
137	instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _
138	
139	@[simp, push ←]
140	lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
141	  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
142	  simp [← Hom.comp_app]
143	
144	end Presheaf
145	
146	noncomputable section Functorial
147	
148	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)
149	
150	/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
151	def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
152	  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom
153	
154	@[simp]
155	lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
156	    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
157	
158	@[simp]
159	lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
160	    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl
161	
162	@[simp]
163	lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
164	    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
165	
166	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
167	def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
168	  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom
169	
170	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
171	is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
172	def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
173	  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _
174	
175	section
176	
177	attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
178	  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts
179	
180	instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
181	instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
182	instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
183	instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
184	
185	end
186	
187	variable (X) in
188	/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
189	to the identity functor. -/
190	def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
191	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
192	
193	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
194	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
195	
196	variable (X) in
197	/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
198	to the identity functor. -/
199	def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
200	  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _
201	
202	variable (X) in
203	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
204	    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
205	      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
206	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _
207	
208	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
209	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
210	def pushforwardComp :
211	    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
212	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _
213	
214	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
215	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
216	
217	/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
218	identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
219	def pullbackComp :
220	    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
221	  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _
222	
223	/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
224	def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
225	    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
226	      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
227	
228	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
229	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
230	
231	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
232	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
233	
234	/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
235	def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
236	  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)
237	
238	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
239	    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
240	      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
241	    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
242	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _
243	
244	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
245	@[reassoc]
246	lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
247	    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
248	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
249	        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
250	    eqToHom (by simp) := by
251	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
252	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
253	  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
254	  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
255	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
256	  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
257	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
258	      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
259	  simp [this]
260	
261	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
262	@[reassoc]
263	lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
264	    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
265	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
266	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
267	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
268	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
269	  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
270	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
271	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
272	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
273	  simp [← this]
274	
275	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
276	@[reassoc]
277	lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
278	    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
279	      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
280	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
281	  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
282	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
283	  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
284	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
285	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
286	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
287	  simp [← this]
288	
289	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
290	attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
291	  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
292	  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
293	/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
294	a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
295	(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
296	these categories.) -/
297	@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
298	  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
299	  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
300	def pseudofunctor :
301	    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
302	  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor

14	
15	A scheme is a locally ringed space such that every point is contained in some open set
16	where there is an isomorphism of presheaves between the restriction to that open set,

42	structure Scheme extends LocallyRingedSpace where

45	      ∃ (U : OpenNhds x) (R : CommRingCat),
46	        Nonempty

14	Given a functor `L : C ⥤ D`, we define the left Kan extension functor

18	the functor `(D ⥤ H) ⥤ (C ⥤ H)` given by the precomposition
19	with `L` (see `Functor.lanAdjunction`).
20	
21	Similarly, we define the right Kan extension functor
22	`L.ran : (C ⥤ H) ⥤ (D ⥤ H)` which sends a functor `F : C ⥤ H` to its
23	right Kan extension along `L`.

And `homEquiv` for the composite:
`(adj₂.comp adj₁).homEquiv F H : (F₂ ⋙ F₁).obj F ⟶ H ≃ F ⟶ (G₁ ⋙ G₂).obj H`
